objective: As a different fresh independent reviewer, re-review the complete HCM-0.1 control pack, retest HCM-R-F-001 through HCM-R-F-006, and close HCM-0.1 only if no actionable finding remains.

sections: Phase 3 — Vocabulary, Context Resolution, Snapshot Memory, and projections

work_completed: Revalidated the complete HCM-0.1 pack against current branch, HEAD, dirty paths, active architecture lineage, live fixed artifact enum/layout and Charter assets, and the absence of the target artifact/intake/posture/Snapshot runtime types.

Retested HCM-R-F-001 successfully: the HCM-0.1 exit row and control-pack proof gate require a completed clean fresh review, and findings require separate remediation plus another different fresh review.

Retested HCM-R-F-002 successfully: Phase 2 is limited to fixed deterministic non-Resolution first-party review renderers, while generic configured custom-kind projections and every Resolution-aware view remain in Phase 3 after HCM-3.2/HCM-3.3.

Retested HCM-R-F-003 successfully: repository closeout uses actual Draft 2020-12 schema validation; negative tests confirmed rejection of a missing required v1.1 object, duplicate ledger ID, record/index mismatch, and byte-rebuild mismatch.

Retested HCM-R-F-004 and found the remediation incomplete: the v1.1 schema and template require snapshot_refs and semantic_refs, but the repository validator does not enforce that v1.0 is historical-only.
Retested HCM-R-F-005 successfully: both bounded SnapshotCapturePolicy windows carry cursors with deterministic null and continuation semantics.
Retested HCM-R-F-006 successfully: ArtifactInstanceDescriptor carries stable schema_id and schema_version identity independently from artifact-kind version.

Reviewed kind/instance/schema ownership, custom-kind safety, intake modes, provenance/candidate/canonical authority, Charter and posture authority, Snapshot integration, shipped-default reservation, sequencing, orchestration, proof, links, portability, archive boundary, and docs-only scope without editing reviewed materials.

deliverables.summary: Immutable v1.1 fresh-review record that leaves HCM-0.1 open and routes one bounded handoff-version admission repair.

decisions.summary: The remediated semantic control pack is coherent across HCM-R-F-001, HCM-R-F-002, HCM-R-F-003, HCM-R-F-005, and HCM-R-F-006, and live runtime truth remains accurately classified as precursors/target-only.
decisions.authority_effect: No semantic landing label or runtime gate changes; all Rust/runtime/open program gates remain open.

decisions.summary: HCM-0.1 does not close because mandatory new-record v1.1 admission is not enforced by the repository validator.
decisions.authority_effect: The HCM-0.1 control-pack proof gate remains open; this review authorizes no Rust and promotes no runtime seam.

findings.summary: HCM-R-F-004 is not fully resolved. The protocol says v1.0 is historical compatibility only and v1.1 is required for every new record, but validate_handoffs.py accepts any record whose schema_version is in the 1.0/1.1 map. In an isolated copy, a newly created v1.0 review record without snapshot_refs or semantic_refs plus an exactly rebuilt ledger passed as 10 records/10 entries. Bounded repair: enforce an immutable historical-v1.0 admission set or equivalent non-spoofable cutover rule and add a negative proof that a new v1.0 record is refused.

blockers.summary: The repository validator does not enforce the protocol's mandatory v1.1 admission rule for new handoffs, so HCM-0.1 cannot satisfy its completed-clean-review gate.
blockers.resume_condition: A separate bounded docs/control-infrastructure remediation makes a newly created v1.0 handoff fail validation, retains all existing v1.0 history as valid, and a different fresh reviewer returns no actionable findings.
blockers.owner: Handbook Contract Membrane orchestration and handoff-protocol authority

validation.command: Live branch/HEAD/dirty-state inspection, exact required Rust/core-asset review, and target-type absence scan
Bounded semantic assertions retesting HCM-R-F-001 through HCM-R-F-006 across 02/04/05/06/08 and the versioned schemas/template
validation.evidence_ref: HCM-R-F-001, HCM-R-F-002, HCM-R-F-003, HCM-R-F-005, and HCM-R-F-006 passed; HCM-R-F-004 failed mandatory new-v1.1 enforcement.

validation.command: Isolated negative validator test adding a new schema_version 1.0 review record without snapshot_refs/semantic_refs and rebuilding the ledger exactly
validation.evidence_ref: validate_handoffs.py returned success: handoff validation passed: 2 record schemas, 1 template, 10 records, 10 ledger entries.

validation.command: Isolated validator negative tests for missing v1.1 semantic_refs, duplicate ledger IDs, record/index mismatch, and byte-rebuild mismatch
validation.evidence_ref: Each malformed isolated copy returned nonzero with the expected validation failure.

Version-routed Draft 2020-12 validation of both schemas, current template, current immutable history, all ledger entries, unique IDs, exact parity, and byte-identical rebuild after this ledger append.

validation.command: Relative-link, portability, active-archive-boundary, and supported-runtime archive checks
validation.evidence_ref: All active targets resolve; active authority does not link into archives; no machine-absolute path appears; supported runtime roots contain no archived references.

validation.command: git status --porcelain=v1 --untracked-files=all docs-only changed-path assertion and git diff --check
validation.evidence_ref: Every changed/untracked path remains under docs/specs/handbook-contract-membrane/, and the working-tree whitespace check passes.

pack_updates.reason: Enforce the documented historical-only v1.0 boundary while retaining current immutable history, and prove that any newly introduced v1.0 record fails validation.

next_session.objective: Select this review finding and dispatch one bounded docs/control-infrastructure remediation that enforces historical-only v1.0 admission, followed by another different fresh independent HCM-0.1 review.
allowed_scope: Revalidate HCM-R2-F-001 and produce one bounded remediation dispatch.
Authorize only the smallest validator/control-protocol proof change needed to reject new v1.0 records while preserving current immutable history.
prohibited_scope: Do not close HCM-0.1 from this review.
Do not rewrite immutable historical handoffs or prior dispatches.
Do not implement Rust, modify tests/core assets/installed skills, select shipped defaults, or start later slices.
Do not widen into unrelated handoff-schema or control-pack cleanup.
acceptance_criteria: The remediation proof retains every current v1.0 record as valid and rejects a newly added v1.0 record even when its schema and ledger entry otherwise validate.
The ordinary full validator remains green with exact parity and byte-identical ledger rebuild.
A subsequent different fresh reviewer reports no unresolved actionable findings before HCM-0.1 closes.
stop_conditions: Stop if the repair would rewrite immutable records or broaden beyond handoff-version admission.
Keep HCM-0.1 open until a different fresh post-remediation review is clean.

chat_summary: Fresh post-remediation review leaves HCM-0.1 open. Five prior findings retest cleanly, but the validator accepts newly created v1.0 records despite mandatory new-record v1.1 policy. One bounded validator-admission repair and another different fresh review are required; no Rust/runtime gate changed.
